title: Teriyaki Chicken Kebabs
description: Tender marinated chicken and colorful vegetables grilled to perfection on skewers. This teriyaki-style kebab recipe features a sweet and savory soy-Sprite marinade that creates juicy, flavorful results every time.
ingredients: 2 pounds boneless skinless chicken breasts, cut into 1 inch chunks
2 bell peppers (any color), sliced into large chunks
1 sweet onion (or purple onion), sliced into large chunks
instructions: Soak wooden or bamboo skewers in water for at least 30 minutes before assembling to prevent burning on the grill.
Make the marinade by combining 1/2 cup soy sauce, 1/2 cup Sprite, 1/2 cup oil, 1 teaspoon garlic powder, and 1 teaspoon horseradish in a large zip-lock bag. Squeeze gently to mix.
Cut 2 pounds chicken into 1-inch chunks and add to the marinade bag. Seal, removing excess air, and massage marinade around chicken. Refrigerate for 1-3 hours (do not exceed 3 hours).

Remove chicken from marinade and discard the used marinade completely. Thread marinated chicken with chopped bell peppers and onion alternately onto skewers, leaving small spaces between pieces for even cooking.
Heat grill to medium-high heat (around 400°F). Lightly grease grates with oil using paper towel and tongs.
Grill kebabs for 5-7 minutes per side, turning once or twice, until chicken reaches internal temperature of 165°F and vegetables have light char marks.
Remove from grill and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
notes: For gluten-free: use tamari instead of soy sauce
Always discard marinade that has touched raw chicken for food safety
Use a meat thermometer to ensure chicken reaches 165°F internal temperature
Do not substitute diet soda for Sprite as artificial sweeteners won't caramelize properly
Cut vegetables and chicken to similar sizes for even cooking
sauce: Teriyaki marinade (soy sauce, Sprite, oil, garlic powder, horseradish)
